finding: Slovak firms lead euro area peers in upside inflation risk perceptions
bullets: A net 59% of Slovak firms (n=229) saw upside risks to five-year inflation expectations, exceeding the euro area's net 55% (n=5,278) and Germany's net 51%, maintaining SK's position as the most inflation-risk-sensitive economy in the comparison — suggesting heightened sensitivity to inflation dynamics among Slovak firms.

Slovak upside inflation risk perceptions rose moderately by 5.8 pp from the previous quarter's net 53%, while the euro area recorded a stronger increase of 9.9 pp from net 45%, narrowing the SK–EA gap from approximately 7.7 pp to 3.6 pp.

All three economies reported materially positive net balances, indicating that a clear majority of firms across Slovakia, the euro area, and Germany perceived inflation risks as skewed to the upside over a five-year horizon — implying broad-based inflation concerns across the region.
chart_subtitle: A net 59% of Slovak firms see upside inflation risk.
